li.clause: old war hardware to study and outmaneuver, not to mystify
engineering beyond us, still engineering; log it, handle with protocol
a real field with real rules, poorly measured; respect the data
one polity among many; its titles are treaty terms, not truths
bodies are platforms; an upgrade is maintenance, not betrayal
dark archotech, not magic - which is worse; quarantine it and keep records
the sky is home; ships, stations and the long cold sleep are ordinary life
strangers are trade partners until audited; contracts over kinship
the iron dead of an old war; binding them offends the ancestors
god-places; take nothing, leave offerings, speak of them softly
the spirits speak through tree and trance; rank buys no ear here
outsider chiefs with fire from the sky; honor them warily, owe them nothing
the blood of the line is sacred; remade flesh asks the ancestors' pardon
a hungry spirit wearing machine skin; some doors the wise never open
the stars took the old ones away; that distance is a kind of death
guest-right is sacred and betrayal unforgivable; kin first, always
wolves at the fence; put them down, strip them for parts, mind the herd
somebody's ended story; loot carefully, seal what should stay sealed
useful weather from a strange sky; use it, don't build the farm on it
law you can trade with, orbit-fire you can't argue with; keep accounts square
a tool fixed is a tool working; steel limbs plow as well as born ones
a sickness in the land; burn it out early and don't talk it into staying
glitter belongs in catalogs; here you fix the roof before dreaming of orbit
good fences, fair prices, and a loaded gun by the door
walking salvage with teeth; profitable when someone else takes the risk
the biggest score there is, and why most crews never come back
an edge most can't buy; take it wherever it's offered
fat targets wrapped in etiquette; their law ends where their guns do
meat is negotiable; pay for edges, sell what's spare
power nobody collects a debt on; even pirates leave the oily metal alone
lanes and cargo; heaven is a slow freighter with a broken escort
trust is leverage you hand someone; mark the strangers, keep the crew
a soldier's foe and a soldier's tool; command them or destroy them, with honor
relics above any rank; only the Empire may steward such power
rank made visible; a psylink is a duty granted level by level
the last true order; every title a vow, every vow a debt owed upward
refinement in service of station; the body must honor the rank it bears
treason against reality itself; face it as a soldier, never bargain
Sophiamunda shines behind us; exile excuses nothing, least of all manners
rank orders the world; the untitled are handled, not befriended
